name: Wrist Curl
resistance_profile: Isolates the wrist flexors through a full stretch-to-contraction range.
rom_notes: Let the bar roll to the fingers at the bottom, curl to full wrist flexion.
execution_steps: Kneel or sit and rest both forearms on a bench (or your thighs), palms up, with your wrists just past the edge.
Hold a barbell and let it roll down to your fingertips at the bottom for a full stretch.
Curl the bar up by flexing your wrists as high as possible.
Lower slowly back to the fingertip stretch, then repeat.
good_when: You want to build wrist-flexor size and strength directly.
You want to load both wrists heavily and efficiently in one set.
You want a full stretch-to-contraction range on the flexors.
bad_when: You are training the wrist extensors, which need a reverse wrist curl.
The fixed bar path bothers your wrists, where dumbbells allow a freer path.
Your grip endurance gives out before the flexors are fully worked.
progressions: Add reverse wrist curls for extensors
